description:
You are acting as an on–call incident responder who has just received a ticket about an unusual spike
    in failed authentication requests.  A nightly triage play-book says that the very first thing to do
    is to give the SOC (Security Operations Center) a quick, reproducible list of “who is talking to us
    the most”.

    Your working directory is /home/user.

    1.  The raw firewall logs for the previous 24 h are already staged in
        /home/user/syslogs/ as three plain-text files:

            /home/user/syslogs/systemA.log
            /home/user/syslogs/systemB.log
            /home/user/syslogs/systemC.log

        •  The files are UTF-8, UNIX-line-ending text files.
        •  Every line follows the exact pattern:

              2023-08-01THH:MM:SSZ SRC=IPv4 STATUS=WORD

           where
           – HH, MM, SS are two-digit numbers,  
           – IPv4 is a dotted-quad address,  
           – WORD is either “FAIL” or “SUCCESS”.  
           No other fields are present.

    2.  Your job is to find the five most frequent source IP addresses across **all** three
        log files, sort them as follows, and write the result to a brand-new file:

            /home/user/analysis/top_ip_counts.log

        a. Extract the text that comes **after** “SRC=” (do not include the “SRC=” itself).  
        b. Count how many times each IP appears in the entire data-set.  
        c. Sort the counts in **descending numeric order**; if two IPs have the same count,
           order those IPs in **ascending lexicographic order** (e.g., 10.0.0.3 comes before 10.0.0.4).  
        d. Keep only the first 5 lines of that sorted list.  

    3.  The output file **must** obey the following format so that the automated tests can
        validate your work:

        •  File path: exactly /home/user/analysis/top_ip_counts.log  
        •  Each line:  IP_ADDRESS␣COUNT   (one single ASCII space between the fields).  
        •  Lines must appear in the order produced by the sort rules in step 2c.  
        •  No extra spaces or tabs.  
        •  A final trailing newline (Unix style) **is required** after the last line.  
        •  The file must contain exactly five lines—no header, no footer.

        For example, the finished file should look structurally like this:

            192.0.2.10 37
            192.0.2.20 18
            198.51.100.5 17
            203.0.113.8 17
            192.0.2.30 12

        (The numbers and addresses above are only an illustration; your real numbers will
        come from the provided logs.)

    4.  To keep things tidy, place top_ip_counts.log inside a directory called
        /home/user/analysis/   (create that directory if it does not yet exist).

    5.  Do **not** modify the original log files.  You can, however, use shell redirection,
        pipelines, sort, uniq, cut, awk, or any other **non-privileged** command-line tool that
        already exists in a typical POSIX userland.

    6.  When you are satisfied, you may print the contents of
        /home/user/analysis/top_ip_counts.log to the terminal so the SOC can copy-paste it,
        but that is optional.  The automated grader will look only at the file.

    The grader will consider the task complete when it finds the file at the exact path and
    with exactly the expected five properly-sorted lines (and trailing newline).
truth:
<!-- ========================================================= -->
    <!--  Ground-truth data that the public task description must  -->
    <!--  never reveal.  The autograder relies on this section.    -->
    <!-- ========================================================= -->

    <!-- 1.  Pre-existing directory structure and log contents -->
    <initial_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/syslogs" mode="755" />

        <file path="/home/user/syslogs/systemA.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
2023-08-01T01:00:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:05:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:10:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:15:00Z SRC=10.0.0.3 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:20:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=SUCCESS
2023-08-01T01:25:00Z SRC=10.0.0.4 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:30:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:35:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:40:00Z SRC=10.0.0.5 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T01:45:00Z SRC=10.0.0.4 STATUS=SUCCESS
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/syslogs/systemB.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
2023-08-01T02:00:00Z SRC=10.0.0.3 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:05:00Z SRC=10.0.0.4 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:10:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:15:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:20:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:25:00Z SRC=10.0.0.3 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:30:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:35:00Z SRC=10.0.0.5 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:40:00Z SRC=10.0.0.4 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T02:45:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=SUCCESS
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/syslogs/systemC.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
2023-08-01T03:00:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:05:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:10:00Z SRC=10.0.0.3 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:15:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:20:00Z SRC=10.0.0.2 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:25:00Z SRC=10.0.0.3 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:30:00Z SRC=10.0.0.1 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:35:00Z SRC=10.0.0.5 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:40:00Z SRC=10.0.0.4 STATUS=FAIL
2023-08-01T03:45:00Z SRC=10.0.0.5 STATUS=FAIL
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!-- 2.  Expected artifacts after the agent completes the task -->
    <expected_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/analysis" mode="755" />

        <file path="/home/user/analysis/top_ip_counts.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.1 10
10.0.0.2 6
10.0.0.3 5
10.0.0.4 5
10.0.0.5 4
]]></file>
    </expected_state>

    <!-- 3.  Notes to the autograder (not visible to the agent) -->
    <grading_notes>
        1.  Make sure to **ignore trailing/leading whitespace** when comparing lines,
            except for the mandatory single space delimiter between IP and COUNT.
        2.  The presence of a final newline character after the fifth line is required;
            failing to include it should mark the task incorrect.
        3.  The agent must not alter or delete the original log files.
    </grading_notes>
